title: dracaena subspicata
intro: Dracaena subspicata, also known as the dragon's blood tree or red-leafed dracaena, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asparagaceae. The plant contains a resinous sap, known as dragon's blood, which has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. Dragon's blood has anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, making it useful in the treatment of various health conditions, including wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The sap can be used to prepare herbal remedies, such as ointments, tinctures, and salves, which have been traditionally used to treat a range of health problems, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues.
